title: The Offensive Playbook Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of exploitation, payloads, and password cracking.

questions.question: What is an 'Exploit' in cybersecurity?
options: A piece of software that takes advantage of a vulnerability to gain access
questions.explanation: Exploits are the tools or code used to cross the threshold into a system.

questions.question: Metasploit is an open-source framework that automates the exploitation process.
questions.explanation: Correct. It is the most popular tool for penetration testing.

questions.question: The code that runs on the target system AFTER a successful exploit is called a ______.
questions.explanation: The payload is the 'cargo' that the exploit delivers.

questions.question: Which type of shell requires the target computer to 'call back' to the hacker's machine?
questions.explanation: Reverse shells are preferred because they bypass many firewall restrictions.

questions.question: What is the goal of 'RCE' (Remote Code Execution)?
options: To run any command on a target machine from a remote location
questions.explanation: RCE gives the attacker full control over the target's command line.

questions.question: Brute-force attacks are much faster than dictionary attacks.
questions.explanation: False. Brute-force tries every possible combination, whereas dictionary attacks only try likely passwords.

questions.question: Which tool uses the power of the GPU (Graphics Card) for incredibly fast password cracking?
questions.explanation: Hashcat is optimized for GPU-accelerated cracking.

questions.question: A hacker leaving a malicious USB drive in a parking lot is performing a ______ attack.
questions.explanation: Baiting exploits human curiosity or greed.

questions.question: What is the main danger of using WEP for your home WiFi?
options: It can be cracked in minutes with simple tools
It only works on old computers
questions.explanation: WEP has fatal mathematical flaws that make it easy to crack.

questions.question: What is an 'Evil Twin' attack?
options: Creating a fake WiFi hotspot with the same name as a legitimate one
questions.explanation: Evil twins are used to trick people into connecting to a malicious hotspot.

questions.question: Post-Exploitation involves activities performed AFTER a hacker has already gained access.
questions.explanation: Yes, this includes stealing data or maintaining access.

questions.question: Metasploit's main command-line interface is called ______.
questions.explanation: msfconsole is the gateway to the framework's power.

questions.question: If you are 'Ethical Hacking', you MUST have ______ before you start.
questions.explanation: The difference between ethical hacking and crime is permission.

questions.question: What is 'Salted' hashing primarily designed to stop?
questions.explanation: Salting ensures every hash is unique, making pre-calculated lists (Rainbow Tables) useless.

questions.question: Once a hacker has a 'Shell', they can run commands as if they were sitting at that computer.
questions.explanation: Correct. A shell is a remote command prompt.

questions.question: In Metasploit, what command would you type to see the details and options of an exploit?
questions.explanation: 'show info' provides descriptions, targets, and required settings.

questions.question: A(n) '______-Day' is a vulnerability that is unknown to the software creator and has no patch.
questions.explanation: Zero-Days are extremely valuable to both hackers and defenders.

questions.question: Why is WPA2-Personal (PSK) vulnerable to a 'Handshake Capture' attack?
options: The way devices connect (handshake) can be captured from the air and cracked offline
questions.explanation: Attackers can capture the encrypted 4-way handshake and attempt to guess the password without being connected to the network.

questions.question: Ethical hackers use the exact same tools and techniques as malicious hackers.
questions.explanation: Yes, but they do it for defense and with permission.

questions.question: When a hacker changes their IP or MAC address to hide their origin, it is called ______.
questions.explanation: Spoofing is the act of pretending to be someone or something else.
